data.title: South Africa trade surplus hits better than expected $1.2 billion in July
data.description:
South Africa recorded a better than expected trade surplus of 20.29 billion rand ($1.16 billion) in July, revenue service figures showed on Friday.

Analyst polled by Reuters had expected a surplus of 18.55 billion rand.

($1 = 17.5632 rand)

data.title: REG - First Property Group - Share purchase by associate
data.description:
RNS Number : 2854X First Property Group PLC 29 August 2025  

Date:

29 August 2025

On behalf of:

First Property Group plc ("First Property", the "Company" or the "Group")

Embargoed:

Immediate Release

First Property Group plc

Share purchase by Associate

First Property Group plc (AIM: FPO), the property fund manager and investor, announces that its Associate, Fprop Phoenix Limited ("PHO"), in which the Group has a 23.78% shareholding, has purchased, today, 100,000 ordinary shares in the Company (the "Ordinary Shares") at a price of 15.00 pence per Ordinary Share.

Following this purchase PHO's beneficial interest in the share capital of the Company is 300,000 Ordinary Shares, equivalent to approximately 0.20% of the Company's total issued share capital.

- ENDS -

For further information please contact:

First Property Group plc             

Tel: +44 (20) 7340 0270

Ben Habib (Chief Executive Officer)

Laura James (Group Finance Director)

Jeremy Barkes (Director, Business Development)

Jill Aubrey (Director, Compliance & Company Secretary)

investor.relations@fprop.com

Allenby Capital (NOMAD & Broker)

Tel: + 44 (20) 3328 5656

Nick Naylor / Daniel Dearden-Williams (Corporate Finance)

Amrit Nahal / Tony Quirke (Sales and Corporate Broking)

Notes to Investors and Editors:

First Property Group plc is a property fund manager and investor with operations in the United Kingdom and Central Europe. Its focus is on higher yielding commercial property with sustainable cash flows.

The Company is flexible and takes an active approach to asset management. Its earnings are derived from:

·            Fund management - via its FCA regulated and AIFMD approved subsidiary, First Property Asset Management Ltd ("FPAM"), which earns fees from investing for third parties in property. FPAM currently manages eleven funds which are invested across the United Kingdom, Poland and Romania.

·            Group properties - principal investments by the Group to earn a return on its own capital, usually in partnership with third parties. These investments currently comprise five directly held properties in Poland, one in Romania, and non-controlling interests in nine of the eleven funds managed by FPAM.

Quoted on AIM, the Company has offices in London and Warsaw. Further information about the Company and its properties can be found at: www.fprop.com. To receive RNS alerts by email please subscribe here.

data.title: South Africa Trade Surplus Shrinks Slightly
data.description:
South Africa recorded a trade surplus of ZAR 20.3 billion in July 2025, down slightly from a revised ZAR 21 billion in the prior month.

Imports jumped by 10.2% to ZAR 164 billion and exports rose 8.5% to ZAR 184 billion.

data.title: HealthEquity Is Maintained at Outperform by Barrington Research
Trump ends security protection for former Vice President Harris, CNN reports
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has canceled Secret Service protection for former vice president and 2024 Democratic rival Kamala Harris, CNN reported on Friday, citing a copy of a letter.

Harris' typical six-month security detail for vice presidents after they leave office had been extended to one year under then-President Joe Biden, CNN reported, citing people familiar with the arrangement. Harris is set to start a book tour for her memoir, "107 Days."

Representatives for the White House and Harris could not be immediately reached for comment on the report.

The letter to Harris, dated Thursday, says the termination of additional security is effective Sept. 1, according to CNN.

A Harris adviser told CNN in a statement that the former vice president was grateful to the U.S. Secret Service for their dedication.

Harris lost the 2024 presidential election to Trump but has not ruled out a possible presidential bid in 2028.

Trump has also ended federal security protection for others, including those who have criticized him such as former national security adviser John Bolton . In March, he ended protection for Biden's children Hunter Biden and Ashley Biden.

data.title: PepsiCo Boosting Stake in Celsius in Deal Worth $585 Million — Update
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

PepsiCo is boosting its stake in energy-drink maker Celsius Holdings in a deal worth $585 million as the companies expand their three-year partnership.

Under the partnership-expansion agreement, Celsius on Friday said it is acquiring the Rockstar Energy brand in the U.S. and Canada from PepsiCo while Celsius's Alani Nu brand will move into PepsiCo's distribution system in North America.

PepsiCo is buying $585 million in newly issued stock to increase its stake in Celsius to 11%.

Shares of Celsius rose 9.2% to $65.18 in premarket trading, which would be a new 52-week high.

Celsius will become PepsiCo's energy-drink lead in the U.S., managing the Celsius, Anani Nu and Rockstar Energy beverage brands, while Purchase, N.Y.-based PepsiCo will lead Celsius's distribution in the U.S. and Canada, said the companies. PepsiCo will continue to own the Rockstar Energy brand internationally, it said.

PepsiCo first invested in Celsius in 2022 for $550 million as it sought to expand its holdings in the energy-drinks market. In 2020, PepsiCo paid $3.85 billion to buy Rockstar Energy.

Celsius has gained market share in a competitive category long dominated by Red Bull and Monster, partly through its image as a healthier alternative. The Boca Raton, Fla., company underwent a new campaign to broaden its consumer base beyond fitness and health and wellness, The Wall Street Journal reported in May.

PepsiCo said it will nominate an additional director to serve on Celsius's board.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: Affirm shares climb as resilient consumer spending drives strong annual forecast
data.description:
Affirm Holdings' shares jumped in premarket trading on Friday, as the company's strong annual forecast for gross merchandise value, driven by resilient consumer spending, eased concerns over its growth after Walmart's impending exit.

In early 2025, Walmart said it would end its six-year-old payments partnership with the buy-now-pay-later firm, with the retail behemoth gradually shifting to its Swedish rival Klarna.

Affirm forecast fiscal year 2026 gross merchandise value (GMV) - the total dollar amount of all transactions on its platform - to be more than $46 billion, above analysts' consensus of $45.67 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

"GMV guidance came in better than expected, which was a particular area of focus by investors as a major enterprise client is going to be removed in FY26," said RBC Capital Markets.

Affirm's robust forecast reflects consumers' increasing use of buy now, pay later options to split payments for big-ticket purchases and ease the immediate financial burden.

"At a category level, general merchandise, electronics, travel and ticketing, and fashion and beauty were significant contributors to overall growth," the company said in its shareholder letter.

A temporary relief from tariff uncertainty and hopes of rate cuts in the back half of 2025 have also driven consumer spending.

"We expect GMV growth will continue at 30%+ year on year and think Affirm can eventually be as big as the traditional point of sale credit card providers," BTIG analysts said in a note.

But the BTIG analysts expect margin pressure in the near term due to increased competition from traditional credit cards and other fintechs.

The company reported a revenue of $876.4 million for the quarter ended June 30, beating estimates of $837 million, driven by strong income from interest.

Affirm's shares were up 15.5% at $92.39 on Friday. If premarket gains hold, the company is set to hit an over three-year high.

The stock has gained nearly 31% so far in 2025 as of last close, outperforming the 11% rise in the broader benchmark S&P 500 index .

At least three brokerages, including J.P.Morgan and Jefferies, have raised their price targets on Affirm's stock after the company's quarterly results.

The median price target of 25 analysts covering the stock is $75 as on Friday, up from $66 in May, and none of them recommend selling Affirm, according to LSEG data.
